1.$description: Top-level surface. Use for the main page background and primary content areas. Do not use bg/2 or bg/3 as hover states on this surface.

2.$description: Recessed or secondary surface. Use behind bg/1 as a page-level backdrop, or on top of bg/1 for small elevated elements like cards. Do not use as a hover color for bg/1.

3.$description: Nested surface. Use only on top of bg/2 for additional layering. Never place directly on bg/1 or use as a hover color for bg/1 or bg/2.

mute.$description: Muted background. Use for subdued, inactive, or disabled surfaces. Not for hover states.

overlay.$description: Overlay background. Use for modals, dialogs, and scrim layers.

pop.$description: Accent background. Use for large card-like areas as a subtle highlight. Place only on bg/1 surfaces — too light for bg/2 or bg/3.

danger.$description: Danger background. Use for large card-like areas as a subtle error or warning highlight. Place only on bg/1 surfaces — too light for bg/2 or bg/3.

warn.$description: Warning background. Use for large card-like areas as a subtle caution highlight. Place only on bg/1 surfaces — too light for bg/2 or bg/3.

safe.$description: Success background. Use for large card-like areas as a subtle positive highlight. Place only on bg/1 surfaces — too light for bg/2 or bg/3.

purple.$description: Purple accent background. Use for large card-like areas as a subtle informational highlight. Place only on bg/1 surfaces — too light for bg/2 or bg/3.

shadow-clr.$description: Shadow color with transparency. Use for drop shadows and elevation effects.

bg.$description: Neutral fill background. Use for small interactive elements like buttons, badges, and tags. Not for cards or large surfaces.

fg.$description: Neutral fill foreground. Use for icons and text on neutral fills.
Accent fill foreground. Use for icons and text on accent fills.

bg.$description: Accent fill background. Use for small interactive elements like primary buttons and action controls. Not for cards or large surfaces.

fg.$description: Danger fill foreground. Use for icons and text on danger fills.

bg.$description: Danger fill background. Use for small interactive elements like destructive buttons and error controls. Not for cards or large surfaces.

fg.$description: Warning fill foreground. Use for icons and text on warning fills.

bg.$description: Warning fill background. Use for small interactive elements like caution buttons and alert controls. Not for cards or large surfaces.

fg.$description: Success fill foreground. Use for icons and text on success fills.

bg.$description: Success fill background. Use for small interactive elements like confirmation buttons and positive controls. Not for cards or large surfaces.

fg.$description: Purple fill foreground. Use for icons and text on purple fills.

bg.$description: Purple fill background. Use for small interactive elements like feature buttons and informational controls. Not for cards or large surfaces.

1.$description: Primary text. Use for headings, body copy, and high-emphasis content.

2.$description: Secondary text. Use for supporting copy, labels, and medium-emphasis content.

3.$description: Tertiary text. Use for placeholders, captions, and low-emphasis content.

1-invert.$description: Inverted primary text. Use for text on dark/accent backgrounds.

2-invert.$description: Inverted secondary text. Use for supporting text on dark/accent backgrounds.

3-invert.$description: Inverted tertiary text. Use for low-emphasis text on dark/accent backgrounds.

pop.$description: Accent text. Use for links, interactive text, and emphasized inline content.

danger.$description: Danger text. Use for error messages and destructive action labels.

warn.$description: Warning text. Use for caution messages and alert labels.

safe.$description: Success text. Use for confirmation messages and positive labels.

purple.$description: Purple accent text. Use for informational labels and feature highlights.

1.$description: Strongest border. Reserved for hover states only — do not use as a default border.

2.$description: Primary border. Use for input fields, cards, and standard dividers.

3.$description: Subtle border. Use for faint separators and background-level divisions.

modification.$description: Change status color for modified items.

addition.$description: Change status color for added items.

deletion.$description: Change status color for deleted items.

rename.$description: Change status color for renamed items.
